Scenario: Scenario 1: Admin invites a new member (one-click)
Precondition: the logged-in user is an admin.
Test data: Admin Email: admin@community.com; New Member Email: newbie@example.com
Steps: the admin enters the new member's email address and clicks Invite.
Expected result: a unique invite token is generated and linked to that email.; an invite email is automatically sent to that address with the registration link containing the token.; the admin sees the invite in the invite list with status "sent".

Scenario: Scenario 2: Admin views all invites and their status
Precondition: the logged-in user is an admin.
Test data: Admin Email: admin@community.com; New Member Email: newbie@example.com
Steps: the admin views the invite management page.
Expected result: the admin sees all invites with status (sent, used, expired, revoked) and the associated member if registered.

Scenario: Scenario 3: Admin revokes an unused invite
Precondition: the logged-in user is an admin.; an invite is unused and not expired.
Test data: Admin Email: admin@community.com; New Member Email: newbie@example.com
Steps: the admin revokes the invite.
Expected result: the invite is marked as revoked and the token can no longer be used.

Scenario: Scenario 4: Cannot invite an email that already belongs to a member
Precondition: the logged-in user is an admin.; an account already exists with the provided email address.
Test data: Admin Email: admin@community.com; New Member Email: newbie@example.com
Steps: the admin attempts to invite that email.
Expected result: the system rejects the invitation.; a message says an account with that email already exists.

Scenario: Scenario 1: Register with a valid invite token
Precondition: an invite token exists and has not been used.; the token has not expired.
Test data: Invite Token: ABC123-XYZ789; Email: newmember@example.com; Password: SecurePass123!; Display Name: Jane Smith
Steps: a new user submits a registration with the token, email, password, and display name (the display name becomes the initial profile display name and can be updated later in profile setup).
Expected result: the user account is created with status EMAIL_PENDING.; a verification email is sent to that email address.; the invite token is marked as used.; the user is shown a message asking them to check their email to verify.

Scenario: Scenario 2: Register with an invalid or already-used invite token
Precondition: an invite token is invalid, expired, or already used.
Test data: Invite Token: ABC123-XYZ789; Email: newmember@example.com; Password: SecurePass123!; Display Name: Jane Smith
Steps: a new user attempts to register with the token.
Expected result: the system rejects the registration.; a message says the invite is invalid.

Scenario: Scenario 3: Register with an email that is already in use (invite token not consumed)
Precondition: an invite token is valid and unused.; an email is already associated with an existing account.
Test data: Invite Token: ABC123-XYZ789; Email: newmember@example.com; Password: SecurePass123!; Display Name: Jane Smith
Steps: a new user attempts to register with the valid invite token and the already-used email.
Expected result: the system rejects the registration.; a message says the email is already in use.; the invite token is NOT marked as used (it remains valid for a different email).

Scenario: Scenario 1: Member with no active reservations requests account deletion
Precondition: the logged-in user is a member.; the member has no REQUESTED, APPROVED, or PICKED_UP reservations as borrower or owner.; the member's tool listings have no REQUESTED, APPROVED, or PICKED_UP reservations on them.
Test data: Password: SecurePass123!
Steps: the member requests account deletion.
Expected result: the account is soft-deleted: the status is set to DELETED, and all personally identifiable information EXCEPT the display name is removed or anonymized (including contact information, profile photo, bio, and neighborhood/location). The display name is preserved for the integrity of past reservation history and reviews.; all ACTIVE tool listings owned by the member are deactivated.; past RETURNED reservation history is preserved.; the member is logged out.; a confirmation email is sent to the registered email address.

Scenario: Scenario 2: Member with active reservations cannot delete account
Precondition: the logged-in user is a member.; the member has one or more REQUESTED, APPROVED, or PICKED_UP reservations as borrower or owner.
Test data: Password: SecurePass123!
Steps: the member attempts to request account deletion.
Expected result: the system rejects the request.; a message lists the active reservations that must be resolved first.; the member is directed to cancel or complete the reservations before trying again.

Scenario: Scenario 3: Account deletion preserves reservation history integrity
Precondition: a member with past RETURNED reservations requests account deletion.
Test data: Password: SecurePass123!
Steps: the account is deleted.
Expected result: past reservations still show in the other party's history (with the deleted member's name and reviews preserved, but other PII removed — contact information, profile photo, bio, and neighborhood/location).; the deleted member's reviews and the ratings they received remain visible to maintain community trust.

Scenario: Scenario 4: Deleted account cannot log in; re-registration requires explicit confirmation
Precondition: a user account has been deleted.
Test data: Password: SecurePass123!
Steps: someone attempts to log in with the deleted account's credentials.; someone attempts to register a new account with the deleted account's email.
Expected result: the system rejects the login.; a generic error appears (account not found).; the system requires the user to confirm by acknowledging the previous account is gone.; the user must obtain a new invite token from an admin to complete registration.; a new account can be created with that email after confirmation.

Scenario: Scenario 5: Suspended member can still request account deletion
Precondition: the logged-in user is a member in SUSPENDED status.; the member has no REQUESTED, APPROVED, or PICKED_UP reservations as borrower or owner.
Test data: Password: SecurePass123!
Steps: the member requests account deletion.
Expected result: the account is soft-deleted following the same rules as Scenario 1.
